_SUPERSEDED_BY: metrics-710.json — this file's codex56/codex55 costs are MCP-contaminated (the -c mcp_servers={} flag did NOT disable the flaky exa MCP; it inflated some codex runs 2-6x). Clean numbers (via a dedicated MCP-free CODEX_HOME) plus opencode/omp columns and 2 more tasks (t9,t10) live in metrics-710.json and report.html v4. Kept for history only.
_note: Hard-task run: 2 bespoke creation tasks (t7-ts-formula, t8-py-query), 3 finalists, 2 runs each. Wall seconds are noisy (concurrent proxy load); cost/tokens are the robust axis. Codex cost via ccusage-codex matched by cwd; ccopus cost from claude -p --output-format json.

tasks.t7-ts-formula: TS spreadsheet formula engine (tokenizer+parser+precedence+functions+strict error taxonomy); Opus calibrated to ~15 min; 61 visible + 44 hidden tests
tasks.t8-py-query: Python record query engine (filter->sort->distinct->paginate->project, strict type semantics); 29 visible + 22 hidden tests

r1.note: only hidden failure: bool-in-ordered-comparison, spec was ambiguous -> not a valid separation; r2 passed

correctness.verdict: tie; the single failure is a spec-ambiguity artifact, honestly flagged and neutralized

confounders_handled: codex MCP servers disabled for runs (dead openaiDeveloperDocs MCP was hanging codex and inflating time/tokens)
wall time noisy from concurrent proxy load -> cost/tokens used as primary efficiency axis
ccopus one transient API drop (Connection closed mid-response) on t8 r1 -> retried once per critic rule
one hidden assertion (bool in ordered comparison) was underspecified -> flagged, spec tightened, not counted as separation

critics.shared_prediction: hard tasks converge to green -> correctness tie -> decision reverts to efficiency where codex55 already wins; confirmed
